summary: G1-G4 PASS, G5 PARTIAL (no .coderabbit/.gemini), e2e DEGRADED (1/4 crates tested, 1 orphan). 1 P0 (orphan), 2 P1. Score 4.4/5.

evidence: 18/18 .github/workflows/*.yml parse; 0 @main / 0 @vN tag rot; 45 SHA-pinned action refs
Org-canonical actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 in 14+ files; trufflehog@75add79b929b263dae147d2e5bcf0daf292165cf SHA + --only-verified --fail
16/18 have top-level `concurrency:` + `permissions:`; ubuntu-24.04 runner in 14/18
windows-surreal.yml: `continue-on-error: true` on cargo test step (accepted, no C++/RocksDB on windows-latest per AGENTS.md)
LICENSE (MIT) + LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE; CHANGELOG.md Keep-a-Changelog 1.1.0 with rich [Unreleased] (coverage, gitleaks, dependabot extension)
CODEOWNERS 2-line `* @KooshaPari` (62-repo org standard); SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, FUNDING.yml, .editorconfig, .gitattributes, .pre-commit-config.yaml, .gitignore all present
AGENTS.md (46 lines, explicit guardrails) + CLAUDE.md + CITATION.cff + Taskfile.yml + Justfile + tarpaulin.toml all present
ISSUE_TEMPLATE/: bug.md, config.yml, feature.md (full set); PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md 24 lines with cargo-specific checklist
docs/adr/0001-record-architecture-decisions.md MADR-formatted; only 1 ADR though (data layer is otherwise undocumented at decision-level)
scorecard.yml (OpenSSF weekly SHA-pinned); codeql.yml + codeql-rust.yml (languages: rust)
secrets-scan.yml: trufflehog SHA + --only-verified; gitleaks.yml: gitleaks/gitleaks-action SHA-pinned (NEW this cycle)
cargo-audit.yml + cargo-deny.yml + cargo-machete.yml + cargo-semver-checks.yml: full Rust supply-chain stack
sbom-refresh.yml present (closes fleet-wide SBOM gap); deny.toml 14-license allowlist; RUSTSEC-2023-0071 ignored with expires:2027-05-05 + rationale
ci.yml: cargo build/test/workspace + clippy -D warnings on ubuntu-24.04; Swatinem/rust-cache SHA-pinned
coverage.yml: cargo-llvm-cov --fail-under-lines 60, lcov -> codecov (closes the 0/40 tarpaulin fleet gap, see tick-25)
tarpaulin.toml mirrors CI floor (informational); pre-commit-config: 6 standard hooks + gitleaks v8.21.2 + cargo-fmt + cargo-check
renovate.json5: group:allNonMajor + vulnerabilityAlerts; dependabot.yml: 3 ecosystems (cargo+npm+github-actions) weekly Monday, grouped with labels
MISSING: .coderabbit.yaml (org standard per tick-19); MISSING: .gemini/ (AGENTS.md mandate, drift)
No branch-protection meta-guard (required-check-names-guard.yml absent); no stale-pr/stale-issue policies
dependabot.yml: 3 ecosystems — fleet standard is 4-6; no gomod/pip/docker (acceptable: pure Rust + VitePress sidecar)
PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md well-structured with cargo checklist; ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ full set
smoke-tests crate has 4 integration tests covering pheno-query (plan_surreal, filter, plan_postgres pagination, crates_are_public) — the only test surface
ZERO tests for pg-bridge and surreal-bridge: only src/lib.rs + src/instrumented.rs; 60% coverage floor sustained by pheno-query alone
fr-coverage.yml: `|| true` + `exit 0` on missing binary — silent non-gate
windows-surreal.yml surreal-bridge test is `continue-on-error: true`; pgvector integration test documented as skip-on-missing-env (AGENTS.md L45)

additional_findings.orphan_crate: crates/surreal-sql-macro/ has src/lib.rs (T38 `query!` proc-macro, compile-time SQL validation, --injection check) but NO Cargo.toml and NOT in [workspace.members] — dead code, not built by `cargo build --workspace`, rots
additional_findings.changelog_quality: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] is rich — best in the data-layer family; serves as a model for sibling repos

actions_recommended.title: Decide on crates/surreal-sql-macro/ — wire it in or delete it
actions_recommended.detail: crates/surreal-sql-macro/src/lib.rs implements a `query!` proc-macro (compile-time SQL validation, --injection check) but has no Cargo.toml and is absent from root [workspace.members]. `cargo build --workspace` does not touch it. Two paths: (1) add Cargo.toml + workspace member + dev-dep on `pheno-query` so `cargo test` exercises it; (2) git rm -r the directory. Choice (1) is correct — the macro closes the documented compile-time SQL-checking gap. Net: closes the orphan rot and adds real test surface to a 4th crate.

actions_recommended.title: Add tests for pg-bridge + surreal-bridge; convert fr-coverage to a real gate
actions_recommended.detail: pg-bridge (lib.rs + instrumented.rs) and surreal-bridge (lib.rs only) have zero tests. Add `crates/pg-bridge/tests/` (mockable deadpool-postgres or trait-based) and `crates/surreal-bridge/tests/` (SurrealDB Mem backend, no C++). Also: fr-coverage.yml:32 `|| true` + L26 `exit 0` on missing binary — replace with `cargo install --git https://github.com/KooshaPari/phenotype-tooling fr-coverage` precondition or delete the workflow. Closes the bulk of the e2e DEGRADED band.

actions_recommended.title: Seed .coderabbit.yaml + .gemini/; add 2nd ADR
actions_recommended.detail: AGENTS.md mandates .coderabbit.yaml (severity info) and .gemini/ (org standard); both missing. Add 2-line .coderabbit.yaml (review_profile: chill, path_filters: ['docs/**','*.md']) and an empty .gemini/ with a brief AGENTS.md cross-link. While there: add docs/adr/0002-data-layer-rationale.md (MADR: Context = SurrealDB embedded vs Postgres+pgvector decision; Decision = dual-stack; Consequences = 2x ops surface) — only ADR 0001 exists. Closes G5 PARTIAL -> PASS.

deep_research: phenoData lands at 4.4/5 against the tick-25 hygiene wave and helioslab-hygiene baseline. Workflow posture is the cleanest in the data-layer family: 45 SHA pins, 0 @main rot, 16/18 concurrency blocks, full Rust supply-chain stack (sbom-refresh + cargo-machete + cargo-semver-checks + cargo-audit + cargo-deny). Governance is exhaustive: dual license, CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md, rich CHANGELOG [Unreleased] (best in family — coverage, gitleaks, dependabot extension, justfile recipes). Security is best-in-class for a maintenance-mode repo (OpenSSF + CodeQL rust + trufflehog SHA + gitleaks + SBOM). The two structural gaps are downstream of 'maintenance' status: (1) orphan `surreal-sql-macro` — proc-macro on disk with no Cargo.toml and not in workspace.members, nothing builds it; (2) only 1 of 4 crates is test-covered (pheno-query via smoke-tests), so the 60% coverage floor is sustained on pheno-query strength alone, masking the bridge-crate gap. fr-coverage.yml is a silent non-gate (`|| true` + `exit 0`), and windows-surreal's `continue-on-error: true` on surreal-bridge means the Windows path is best-effort (acceptable per AGENTS.md L45).
deep_research_cont: Three concrete follow-ups: wire `surreal-sql-macro` into the workspace (preferred) or delete it — T38 proc-macro is a real asset; add integration tests for pg-bridge (mockable) and surreal-bridge (Mem backend) and either fix fr-coverage to be a real gate with `cargo install` precondition or remove it; seed .coderabbit.yaml + .gemini/ per AGENTS.md and add a 2nd MADR ADR for the SurrealDB-vs-Postgres dual-stack decision. The CHANGELOG is the model to seed in sibling repos. After P0 + 2 P1 fixes, phenoData moves from 'mostly compliant with one structural rot and a coverage gap' to 'fully compliant, e2e DEGRADED -> PARTIAL' and can serve as the data-layer hygiene baseline for any future repo wrapping SurrealDB or pgvector.
